inclusion_criteria: Randomized controlled trials with a control condition (standard care, waitlist, or no intervention).
Adults (≥18 years) with overweight or obesity (BMI ≥25 kg/m²) who have comorbid depressive symptoms at baseline (clinical diagnosis or elevated score on a validated depression scale).

Internet- or mobile-based interventions delivered via web browser or smartphone app aimed at improving mental health (e.g., CBT, mindfulness), delivered predominantly digitally (stand-alone or digitally guided).
Report quantitative outcomes for both depressive symptom severity and weight-related measures (body weight or BMI) at post-intervention and/or follow-up.
exclusion_criteria: Non-randomized designs (e.g., quasi-experimental, single-arm, observational), feasibility studies without randomization, protocols, or conference abstracts.
Populations not meeting both criteria: adults with BMI ≥25 and comorbid depressive symptoms (e.g., children/adolescents, normal-weight samples, absence of depressive symptoms).

Interventions not delivered primarily via web/app (e.g., face-to-face psychotherapy, telephone/SMS-only programs, wearables/devices without a therapeutic app) or apps focused solely on weight management without explicit mental health content.
Do not report both primary outcomes (depressive symptom severity and weight/BMI) as quantitative measures.
screening_criteria.description: Single-layer screen focusing on RCTs of web- or app-based mental health interventions in adults with overweight/obesity and baseline depressive symptoms, requiring both depressive symptom and weight/BMI outcomes.

included_articles.title: Impact of a self-guided, eHealth program targeting weight loss and depression in men: A randomized trial.
